name: Red Snapper Veracruzana (Huachinango a La Veracruzana)
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup olive oil or less as desired
3/4 cup pimento-stuffed green olives, finely chopped
2 sprigs fresh thyme or 1 teaspoon each dried
2 sprigs fresh marjoram or 1 teaspoon each dried
recipeInstructions:
1. In a blender or food processor, blend lime juice, garlic, bay leaves, oregano, oil, salt, and pepper to make a marinade.
2. Place the fish in a large baking dish and pour the marinade over it. Cover and marinate for about 1 hour in the refrigerator.
3. Prepare the tomato sauce: Heat olive oil in a saucepan. Brown 3 garlic cloves and discard them. Mince the remaining garlic and brown it with the onion in the oil. Add tomatoes, olives, capers, bay leaves, oregano, thyme, marjoram, salt, pepper, and chilies. Simmer for 2 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally.
4. Remove from heat and set aside.
5. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Remove the fillets from the marinade and place them in another large baking dish. Bake until the fish is done, about 15 minutes, basting with some of the tomato sauce occasionally. Do not overcook.
6. To serve, place the fillets on a platter and cover with the remaining sauce, which has been warmed. Any extra sauce can be served on the side, accompanied by white rice. Makes 4 servings.
